dishes.name: Guavacation
dishes.raw_description: We added Guava to this traditional Berliner Weisse; a cloudy sour beer originating in Northern Germany that’s creation dates back to the 16th century. Low ABV, low bitterness and soured with a lactobacillus strain, it is vibrantly tart as well as amply carbonated. Fun fact about this style: Napoleon referred to it as the “Champagne of the North”–and we think it pairs well with Orlando’s vibe.
dishes.reasoning: Beer may be fined with isinglass or gelatin; vegan status uncertain. | evidence: “Berliner Weisse with guava”

dishes.name: Halftoberfest Vienna Lager

dishes.name: Urayasu Rice Lager
dishes.raw_description: Urayasu Rice Lager is a Japanese-inspired beer, named after the Sister City of Orlando, FL and where another well-known amusement park calls home today. Our connection with the History in Orlando brings back the past of a previous amusement park, where this one brings back their history of being an important fishing village for the Edo capital. Rice adds clarity, crispness, and a slight sweetness, while still only making up less than one third of the grain bill. This well-balanced beer combines the earthy herbal flavors of the land with the nobility and brightness of the sea, as Urayasu has.
dishes.reasoning: Beer may be fined with isinglass or gelatin; vegan status uncertain. | evidence: “Japanese Rice Lager”
